title: Try a new way to initialize the CUDA runtime

body:
This PR attempts to clarify how the code reads by exchanging the term "runoff" for "auxiliary_freshwater_flux". The point is that what we call "runoff" is not really specific to runoff at all (and besides, it also applies to icebergs which is not "runoff"). 

Rather the fluxes that are now called "runoff" actually allows users to implement prescribed atmospheres that have two kinds of freshwater flux --- one on the "main" grid common to other atmospheric variables, and a second one on a "different" grid (also with different times).
title: Generalize "runoff" to any "auxiliary" freshwater flux for clarity
